prompt: Create a polished horizontal workflow diagram for a project called 'Paper Leaderboard Pipeline'. Goal: explain the workflow clearly for a GitHub README or project homepage, and make the process feel rigorous, reusable, and engineering-oriented. Diagram content, left to right: 1. Search keyword design 2. arXiv retrieval 3. Google Scholar citation lookup via Serper API 4. Ranking and scoring 5. LLM keyword extraction from title and abstract 6. Static leaderboard frontend. Requirements: six clearly separated stages, concise title per stage, simple icon-like visual per stage, arrows connecting all stages, strong pipeline structure, readable in a README. Style: modern technical infographic, clean white or very light background, dark text, restrained teal / slate / muted gold accents, no clutter, no cartoon style, no pseudo-code blocks. Visual hints per stage: keyword design = magnifier plus keyword chips; arXiv retrieval = paper stack; Serper citation lookup = scholar search and citation badge; ranking = sorted bars or leaderboard; LLM keyword extraction = abstract text and tag chips; frontend = browser window and table UI. Output intent: crisp professional static explanatory diagram for documentation.
